Title: Judgement of Heaven - Live
Comment:
Nuclear take: Blaze is my favourite Maiden vocalist. It's not his fault he happened to be in the band for two stinkers of albums, is it? 

The songs in The X Factor and Virtual XI aren't the best but the production is what kills them. Even the 2015 remasters sound shite. Why is the bass so high? Why'd they let Harris do that? Either way, Maiden fans fucking hated Blaze and his albums. 

But I love how Blaze has taken ownership of his tracks with Maiden. I saw him perform The X Factor in full last year and the crowd went fucking wild for Judgement of Heaven. In that moment I knew I was in a room full of real connoisseurs.

There's a wonderful acoustic version of it from an album called Nylon Maiden but I picked this allegedly live version because it captures the Brummie essence of Blaze.

Title: The Spine (feat. Ashley Barrett)
Comment:
(Taken from the Transistor Video game Wiki page)
Red is the player character and one of the two main protagonists of Transistor. Along with her companion the titular Transistor.

Red was a well-known performing artist in the city of Cloudbank before her voice was stolen in a terrifying incident after one of her shows.
In the context of the game, all vocal songs in the Soundtrack were written and performed by Red.

In the game, "the spine" was originally written by Red and has been a well-liked part of her repertoire. It is first heard as part of the soundtrack when Red examines a promotional poster of herself in Goldwalk Promenade, pining for her performance days and unable to speak. Her companion Says "Look, whatever you're thinking, do me a favor; don't let go."

Red can also play the song in the Sandbox and play it on the Music Player; upon hearing it there, Her companion remarks that it is "a classic."
